name: EMQX
what_it_does: High-performance MQTT message broker with a REST API for fleet management, rule engine configuration, and real-time message routing.
use_cases: Managing large IoT device fleets and monitoring connection status via REST API
Publishing messages to MQTT topics from an agent to trigger device actions
Configuring rule engines to route IoT messages to databases or webhooks
Monitoring broker statistics and client sessions programmatically
Building agents that react to real-time sensor data from connected devices
not_for: General-purpose team chat or human messaging use cases
Workloads requiring persistent message queues with guaranteed delivery ordering (use Kafka instead)
best_when: Best when you need to manage large-scale IoT device communication at high concurrency, especially when MQTT is the required protocol.
avoid_when: Avoid when your use case is standard HTTP API messaging without IoT device constraints, or when you need Kafka-style log retention.

auth.notes: REST API uses HTTP Basic Auth or API key. MQTT connections support username/password and TLS client certificates.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free self-hosted (open-source); no limits on connections
pricing.paid_tiers: EMQX Cloud from $0.18/hour; EMQX Enterprise with SLA from custom pricing
pricing.notes: Open-source version handles 100M+ MQTT connections. Cloud version adds managed infrastructure and SLA.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no REST API rate limits by default. EMQX Cloud: plan-dependent connection limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with error codes. MQTT protocol errors are less descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: MQTT publish is not idempotent at QoS 0; QoS 1 and 2 provide at-least-once and exactly-once delivery.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API path and structure changed significantly between v4 and v5 — verify version
MQTT QoS level must be chosen carefully; QoS 0 drops messages under load
Rule engine configuration via API requires understanding of EMQX's SQL-like rule syntax

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS mutual authentication supported for MQTT. ACL-based access control for topic-level permissions. Active security audit history.
